properties.name: Mission San Juan Bautista
properties.summary: 1797. Moraga 1806 departure; Viader 1810 Aug return.

properties.name: Mission San Fernando
properties.summary: 1797. Moraga 1806 arrival.

properties.name: Mission San José
properties.summary: 1797. Viader/Moraga 1810 departure point.

properties.name: Mission Santa Clara
properties.summary: 1777. Viader 1810 Oct return.

properties.name: Mission San Miguel
properties.summary: 1797. Moraga 1807 stop.

properties.name: Monterey Presidio
properties.summary: Moraga 1807 departure.

properties.name: Mission San Rafael
properties.summary: 1817. First establishment north of SF Bay. Site selected by Moraga. Founded 83 days after Hagemeister-Pomo treaty.

properties.name: Fort Ross
properties.summary: 1812. Russian-American Company. Kuskov. 95 Russians + ~80 Aleut hunters. Moraga visited 1812, 1813, 1814.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: 1776. Founded by José Joaquín Moraga (Gabriel's father). Gabriel served here as Alférez 1806–1818.

properties.name: Monterey
properties.summary: Departure. 25 cavalry under Alférez Gabriel Moraga. Sgt. Miguel Espinosa. 8 horses each.

properties.name: Mission San Miguel
properties.summary: 'We went from Monterey to San Miguel Mission.'

properties.name: Buena Vista Lake
properties.summary: 'From there to the Laguna we called Buena Vista in one day and a half.' Went after runaway neophytes — 'did not get any.'

sources.citation: Cook note 46: 'This expedition of 1807 is otherwise unrecorded.'

properties.name: Mountains — Salinas de Cortéz
properties.summary: 'We went away into the Snowy Mountains.' Indians stole half the horses and killed two men. 'Portezuelo' with great quantities of nitre.

sources.citation: Cook note 47: possibly entrance to Gorge of Kern R., E of Bakersfield

properties.name: San Joaquin River (crossings)
properties.summary: 'We crossed the San Joaquin River several times and everywhere there was Indians.'

properties.name: Mission San José
properties.summary: 'The Captain made up his mind to go back by the way of San Jose Mission where we arrived in good order.'

properties.name: Mission San José
properties.summary: Aug 15, 1810. Departed 5:30pm with Lt. Moraga, Cadet Estrada, 1 corporal, 3 soldiers, 4 neophytes.

properties.name: Valley of San José — Pool
properties.summary: Aug 15. 6 leagues N. 'Good water. So near and so well known to everyone.'

properties.name: Walnut Creek
properties.summary: Aug 16 (lunch). 6 leagues N. 'Good water but runs in very small amount.' Killed 2 bears and a deer.

sources.citation: Cook note 1: route via Sunol, Dublin, Walnut Creek

properties.name: End of Walnut Creek — Estuaries
properties.summary: Aug 16. 6 more leagues N. 'Beginning of some estuaries on the NE side of a fine plain.' Big walnut trees.

properties.name: San Joaquin R. / Tulpunes — Antioch
properties.summary: Aug 18. 7 leagues E. 'Quarter-league wide, apparently very deep, reached by tides.' Large oak forest. Tulpunes not seen.

sources.citation: Cook note 2: at or near Antioch. Julpunes oak forest to Brentwood.

properties.name: Oak Forest — Oakley
properties.summary: Aug 18. 2 more leagues E. Good country, no water except the river.

sources.citation: Cook note 2: halt near Oakley

properties.name: Pescadero / Cholvones — Bethany
properties.summary: Aug 19. 10 leagues SE. Lake in oak grove. Interpreter met heathen Guanats. 'Christian fugitives from San Jose on opposite shore.'

sources.citation: Cook note 3: Bethany. Lakes vanished.

properties.name: Tomchom Village
properties.summary: Aug 20. 3 leagues SSE. 'No wild Indian showed himself.'

sources.citation: Cook note 4: north of Tracy

properties.name: Cuyens Village
properties.summary: Aug 20. 2½ leagues further. Chief Cuyens. 15 Indians brought fish. 4 stayed the night.

sources.citation: Cook note 4: left bank, 3 mi above highway bridge

properties.name: Maijem Village
properties.summary: Aug 21. 2½–3 leagues. Dry stream bed. Indians came 'with great trepidation' then disappeared.

sources.citation: Cook note 5: ~2 mi NNE of Vernalis

properties.name: Opposite Bozenats Village
properties.summary: Aug 21. 2 leagues. Indians on far bank 'refused to come near, yelled with much vigor.'

sources.citation: Cook note 6: right bank, E of Vernalis

properties.name: SKIRMISH — Tationes / Apaglamenes
properties.summary: Aug 22. 2 leagues + 1 league. 30 armed heathen on opposite bank. Then Tationes village. Apaglamenes and Tationes attacked — 'painted, bedecked with feathers.' 12 shots fired. One Indian hit.

quote.en: 'Soldier Morales came into the skirmish with half his chin shaved.'

sources.citation: Cook note 7: ~2 mi SE of Grayson

properties.name: Lakes near Willow Grove
properties.summary: Aug 22 afternoon. 3 leagues. 'Neither firewood nor good land.'

sources.citation: Cook note 8: probably E of Patterson

properties.name: Creek — Orestimba
properties.summary: Aug 23. 3½ leagues. 'Great heat forced us to jump in for a swim.'

sources.citation: Cook note 9: Orestimba Cr., E of Crows Landing

properties.name: Pond — NE of Gustine
properties.summary: Aug 23. 4 leagues. 'No wood or brush to cook supper or even make chocolate.'

sources.citation: Cook note 10: 3–4 mi NE of Gustine. Southernmost point.

properties.name: San Luis Gonzaga
properties.summary: Aug 24. Turned back W! 10 leagues. Explored — 'no more water than a few pools.' Not suitable for mission.

properties.name: Ausaymas Creek
properties.summary: Aug 26. SW over mountains. 'Caught forty fish including six trout.' Already the plain of San Juan Bautista.

properties.name: Mission San Juan Bautista
properties.summary: Aug 27. 5 leagues SW. 'With a good breakfast we brought the expedition to an end.' Signed Fr. José Viader, Aug 28, 1810.

properties.name: Mission San José
properties.summary: Oct 19, 1810. Left ~2pm with Lt. Moraga, 23 soldiers, ~50 armed Christian Indians.

properties.name: Valley of San José
properties.summary: Oct 19. 6 leagues NE. Willow grove with good water.

properties.name: Pescadero / Cholvones — Bethany
properties.summary: Oct 20. 9 leagues ENE. 'Arrived very late so as not to be seen or heard. Placed our people in position to attack a dance.'

sources.citation: Cook note 11: Mission San José to Bethany = ~32 mi

properties.name: Bolbones Villages — Old River
properties.summary: Oct 21. 'Before dawn we assaulted a village.' Captured 15 San Jose Christians, 18 heathen men, 51 women. Women released — 'went away very happy.' Two prisoners escaped.

sources.citation: Cook note 12: two villages on Old River / Union I.

properties.name: Jusmites
properties.summary: Oct 22. 2½ leagues SE along river. 'Oak groves, willow thickets, ponds.'

sources.citation: Cook note 14

properties.name: Tugites
properties.summary: Oct 22. 2½ more leagues. 'All the country has wood, water in the river and lakes, but the land is low, flooded.'

sources.citation: Cook note 14

properties.name: Cuyens Village
properties.summary: Oct 23. Met friendly Cuyens Indians. 'Bearing as a gift three very big, red, salmon.'

properties.name: Mayem Village
properties.summary: Oct 23. 4 leagues total. 'Approached us with much fear and a tendency to prevaricate.' Deny harboring Christians. Lt. threatens flogging.

sources.citation: Cook note 14: Mayem 9 leagues from Pescadero

properties.name: Ford Opposite Taualames
properties.summary: Oct 23 afternoon. 2 leagues. 'Ford across the river — very bad, wide and deep.' Crossed without incident. Sent boy to village.

sources.citation: Cook note 14–15

properties.name: Toward Tuolumne — Blocked
properties.summary: Oct 24. Scouted N toward Tuolumne (Dolores), 2–3 leagues away. 'On account of so many sloughs, swamps, and ponds we turned back.'

properties.name: Past Apelamenes & Tatives
properties.summary: Oct 24 afternoon. 6 leagues SSE. 'Passed in front of the Apelamenes and Tatives Indians, who had fought us on the former occasion.'

properties.name: Merced River
properties.summary: Oct 24. Reached Merced R. 'Almost swimming the horses.' Much wood — oak, cypress, cottonwood.

sources.citation: Cook note 15

properties.name: Crossed San Joaquin
properties.summary: Oct 25. SW. 'Crossed several other swampy sloughs. Southward no more trees, only tules.'

properties.name: 'Battle / Shaving Place'
properties.summary: Oct 25. 2½ leagues downstream. 'At this point now, as previously, the soldiers shave themselves.'

properties.name: Orestimba Creek — Apalamenes
properties.summary: Oct 25. 1 league. 'Allies of the Tatives when they fought us.' Creek dry. 'Least bad spot — only firewood, river water, and good fish.'

sources.citation: Cook note 9/16

properties.name: Opposite Taualames (return)
properties.summary: Oct 25. 2 leagues. 'Refused to come out, saying they were afraid. They guessed right for they would have been taken captive.'

properties.name: Del Puerto Creek — Corpus Christi
properties.summary: Oct 25. 3 leagues W. 'Spent the night without water for the horses. Had to dig a well in the sand.'

sources.citation: Cook note 16: Del Puerto Cr.

properties.name: El Toro
properties.summary: Oct 26. 6 leagues W. Mountains, bad trails. Lunch stop.

properties.name: San Guillermo
properties.summary: Oct 26. 6 more leagues W. 'Extremely bad trail.' Pools of good water.

properties.name: Old Village of Patenos
properties.summary: Oct 27. 6 leagues W. Lunch stop.

sources.citation: Cook note 16: through hills NE of Mt. Hamilton

properties.name: Mission Santa Clara
properties.summary: Oct 27. 5 leagues NW. 'In good condition, thanks be to God.' Signed Fr. José Viader, Oct 28, 1810.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: May 1810. Governor Arrillaga orders Moraga to pursue the Suisun, who have killed 16 Christians over 3 years. Moraga departs with 17 men.

sources.citation: Arrillaga report, Prov. Rec. ix. 122–3 (C-A 25).

properties.name: Overland March — East Bay Hills
properties.summary: Route overland from the Presidio, skirting the south shore of the Bay toward the Strait. Exact route unknown but likely through modern Oakland/Richmond hills.

sources.citation: No diary survives for this expedition.

properties.name: Carquinez Strait Crossing
properties.summary: Troops had no boats. Found a single small vessel. 'With great sacrifice, the Ensign embarked his troops and horses, making several crossings.' Multiple trips across the narrows.

quote.es: 'Las tropas no tienen lanchas; que se encontró una muy chica en la que con sacrificios se embarcó el Alferez su tropa y caballada, haciendo varios viajes.'

sources.citation: Arrillaga report. Vallejo calls it the 'Karkin-aku pass.'

properties.name: Skirmish on Suisun Creek
properties.summary: Initial engagement. After crossing the strait, troops fought Suisun warriors along the banks of a creek. Vallejo (via Sánchez): the Suisun chief drew the Spanish inland toward the main body of warriors. Sgt. Francisco Soto left as rear guard with horses.

quote.en: 'After defeating the Indians on the banks of the Suisun River... pursued them to their last stronghold.'

sources.citation: Vallejo, Recuerdos Históricos, i. 131–5. Vallejo attributes to Sánchez (1817) but likely describes 1810.

properties.name: Battle at Malaca's Ranchería
properties.summary: Main engagement, May 22, 1810. Captured 18 gentiles (released — too wounded to transport). Survivors retreated into 3 tule huts. 2 corporals (Herrera, Soto) and 2 soldiers (Briones, Zúñiga) wounded. After 2 huts stormed, 3rd set on fire. Defenders burned alive rather than surrender. ~120 Suisun fighters. Chief Malaca's speech: 'It is better to die free than to live as slaves, despised by our Puy.'

quote.en: 'Los valientes indios murieron envueltos en las llamas antes que entregarse.' — Arrillaga

sources.citation: Bancroft II, p. 91 fn. 24. Arrillaga: Prov. Rec. ix. 122–3. Royal Order: Prov. St. Pap. xix. 314. Vallejo: MS. i. 131–5.

properties.name: Return Crossing — Carquinez Strait
properties.summary: Vallejo: 'At dawn they tried to ford a creek and came back over the Carquines Strait.' Return to south shore after the battle.

sources.citation: Vallejo, Recuerdos, i. 131–5.

properties.name: Return to Presidio
properties.summary: Moraga returns to the Presidio. Arrillaga reports to the Viceroy on June 20, 1810. Royal Order arrives Nov. 12, 1811: Moraga promoted to brevet Lieutenant of Cavalry.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: October 3, 1810. Governor orders Moraga to investigate Russian fur-hunting at Bodega Bay. 10-day reconnaissance.

sources.citation: Langelier & Rosen, fn. 52: 'Gabriel Moraga's Diary of his Expedition to the Port of Bodega, Oct. 3, 1810, Prov. St. Pap., XII, 276–79.' Diary survives in the Archives of California.

properties.name: Bay Crossing — to Marin
properties.summary: Crossed the bay from the Presidio to the Marin shore. Method uncertain — possibly native balsas or a borrowed vessel.

sources.citation: SF Presidio had limited boat access. See boat-problem article.

properties.name: San Rafael Area
properties.summary: Passed through future site of Mission San Rafael. Bancroft: 'The site was probably selected on the advice of Moraga, who had several times passed it on his way to and from Bodega.'

sources.citation: Bancroft, II, p. 330.

properties.name: Novato / Olompali Area
properties.summary: Route north through Marin. Approximate — following the natural corridor through the Novato gap toward Petaluma Creek and the coast.

sources.citation: Exact route not specified in diary abstract.

properties.name: Petaluma Creek / Estuary
properties.summary: Likely crossed or followed Petaluma Creek toward the coast. Natural route from interior Marin to Bodega Bay.

sources.citation: Route reconstruction based on geography.

properties.name: Bodega Bay
properties.summary: Arrived at Bodega Bay. Found an American ship and Alaskan Indians (Aleut hunters working for the Russian-American Company). 10-day reconnaissance of the area. Wrote a diary of the expedition.

sources.citation: Langelier & Rosen: 'finds an American ship and Alaskan Indians.' Diary: Prov. St. Pap., XII, 276–79.

properties.name: Return to Presidio
properties.summary: Returns after 10 days. Report confirms Russian presence and fur-hunting operations at Bodega.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: July 1812. Argüello sends Moraga with 4 men to investigate construction of a small Russian fort on the Sonoma coast. Fort Ross had been dedicated Sept. 10, 1812 — but Kuskov had been building since spring.

sources.citation: Langelier & Rosen, Ch. 3.

properties.name: Bay Crossing — to Marin
properties.summary: Crossed the bay to the Marin shore.

properties.name: San Rafael Area
properties.summary: Through future Mission San Rafael site. Moraga's repeated passage through here informed his later recommendation of the site.

properties.name: Petaluma Creek Area
properties.summary: North through Marin toward the coast. Approximate route.

properties.name: Bodega Bay
properties.summary: Passed through or near Bodega Bay en route to Fort Ross.

properties.name: Fort Ross
properties.summary: Moraga identifies the Russian settlement: a brigantine-anchored base with a 150-yard-square stockade and cannon. Notes the Russians' friendly manner. Fort Ross had been dedicated by Kuskov with 95 Russians and ~80 Aleut hunters.

sources.citation: Langelier & Rosen: '150-yard-square stockade and cannon; notes the Russians' friendly manner.'

properties.name: Return to Presidio
properties.summary: Returns to SF. Report filed with Argüello.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: January 1813. Moraga drives 20 cattle and 3 horses to Fort Ross — practical diplomacy. Returns again in 1814 with Cadet Gervasio Argüello.

sources.citation: Langelier & Rosen, Ch. 3.

properties.name: Bay Crossing — to Marin
properties.summary: Crossed bay to Marin shore with livestock.

properties.name: San Rafael Area
properties.summary: Through future mission site, driving cattle north.

properties.name: Bodega Bay Area
properties.summary: Through Bodega country toward Fort Ross.

properties.name: Fort Ross
properties.summary: Jan. 1813: Delivered 20 cattle, 3 horses as goodwill gesture. 1814 return: accompanied by Cadet Gervasio Argüello. Wrote a report describing 40 gente de razón and many Kodiak Indians, cannon, glass windows in the commandant's house. Also carried official demands for Russian withdrawal.

sources.citation: Langelier & Rosen: '40 gente de razón and many Kodiak Indians, cannon guarding the place, and glass windows in the commandant's house.'

properties.name: Return to Presidio
properties.summary: Returns to SF Presidio.

properties.name: Presidio of San Francisco
properties.summary: c. 1817. Moraga reconnoiters the site north of the Bay for a new establishment. He had passed through this area multiple times on trips to Bodega and Fort Ross since 1810.

sources.citation: Bancroft II, p. 330: 'The site was probably selected on the advice of Moraga.'

properties.name: Bay Crossing — to Marin
properties.summary: Crossed the bay to the Marin shore. SF Presidio had no proper boat from Feb. 1816 to Nov. 1818 — crossing method uncertain. Possibly native tule balsas.

sources.citation: See boat-problem article. Vallejo mentions 'a small boat belonging to the San Francisco presidio.'

properties.name: Mission San Rafael Site
properties.summary: Moraga selects this site — 5 leagues north of the Presidio, on the other side of the Bay. Warmer climate, near homeland of many SF neophytes. Mission founded Dec. 14, 1817 under Fr. Luis Gil y Taboada, escorted by Cpl. José Herrera (wounded at Suisun 1810, promoted to Sgt.). Vallejo says the Suisun victory led directly to the decision to found the mission.

quote.es: 'Con la mira tambien de atraer los muchos gentiles que existen por allí.' — Solá to Viceroy, April 3, 1818

sources.citation: Solá letter: Prov. Rec. ix. 583–604 (C-A 25). Founded 83 days after Hagemeister-Pomo treaty.

properties.name: Return to Presidio
properties.summary: Returns to the Presidio with his recommendation. Governor Solá approves the plan in consultation with Prefect Sarría.
